title: Sticky global navigation remains fixed at top on scroll (desktop)
description: Verify the header/navigation remains fixed to the top of the viewport when the user scrolls down the homepage on a desktop viewport.

steps.description: Open the homepage (index.html) in a desktop viewport (e.g., 1366x768).
Note the initial position of the global navigation/header.
Scroll down the page by several screen heights.
Check that the header element has CSS position fixed or sticky and remains visible at the top of the viewport during and after scrolling.
Confirm header content (logo, primary nav links) are still accessible and clickable while sticky.

title: Sticky global navigation behavior on mobile with burger menu
description: Verify the header stays sticky and the mobile burger menu opens/closes and remains accessible while scrolling on a mobile viewport.

steps.description: Open the homepage on a mobile viewport (e.g., 375x812).
Ensure the burger menu icon is visible in the header.
Tap the burger menu to open the navigation panel.
Confirm the navigation panel is visible, links are present, and focus is set to the first menu item or close control.
Scroll the page while the menu is open.
Verify the header remains sticky and the open menu does not detach or reposition incorrectly; menu close control remains usable.
Tap the close control or the burger icon again to close the menu.
Confirm the menu closes and focus returns to the burger control.

title: Navigation keyboard accessibility and focus states
description: Verify that the global navigation and burger menu are operable via keyboard and have visible focus indicators complying with accessibility checklist.

steps.description: Open the homepage on desktop.
Use Tab to navigate to header controls, including logo, nav links and (on narrow viewport) the burger menu.
Ensure each interactive element receives keyboard focus and a visible focus outline or style is present.
On mobile/narrow layout, open the burger menu via keyboard (Enter/Space) and navigate menu items using Tab/Shift+Tab and Arrow keys if supported.
Confirm keyboard focus is trapped within the open menu until it is closed and that pressing Escape closes the menu and returns focus to the burger control.

title: Testimonial slider autoplay, loop and author details
description: Verify the testimonial slider auto-plays, advances slides at regular intervals, loops infinitely, and displays correct author name, role (if present) and image with alt text.

steps.description: Open the homepage where the testimonial slider is embedded.
Observe the slider for at least three intervals (e.g., wait for 3 x configured interval length).
Verify the slider advances automatically at consistent intervals without user interaction.
After reaching the last testimonial, confirm the slider loops back to the first testimonial and continues playing (infinite loop).
For each visible testimonial, confirm the author name text is present and an author image is shown with non-empty src and proper alt attribute describing the author.

title: Testimonial slider keyboard and reduced motion behavior
description: Ensure the slider can be controlled by keyboard (prev/next if controls exist) and respects user's prefers-reduced-motion setting by disabling auto-play/animation when enabled.

steps.description: Open the homepage and simulate a user with 'prefers-reduced-motion: reduce'.
Confirm the slider does not auto-play or animate; content remains static or transitions are disabled.
Restore normal motion preference. Focus on slider controls via keyboard and attempt to trigger next/previous using Enter/Space or arrow keys (depending on implementation).
Confirm keyboard controls move slides and announce change focus appropriately (or set aria-live updates) so screen reader users can detect slide changes.

title: Appointment form - required fields validation (client-side)
description: Verify that the appointment booking form validates required fields and shows inline validation messages before submission.

steps.description: Open appointment.html or the embedded booking form on the homepage.
With all fields empty, click the form Submit button.
Confirm client-side validation triggers and inline error messages are shown for each required field (name, contact, service selection, date/time as applicable).
Fill only some required fields and submit again.
Verify validation still blocks submission and highlights the remaining missing fields with appropriate messages.

title: Appointment form - email, phone and date validation edge cases
description: Confirm the form validates email format, phone number format, and date/time rules (no past dates) and provides clear error messages.

steps.description: Open the appointment form.
Enter an invalid email (e.g., 'user@@example', 'user.com') and attempt to submit.
Check the form shows an invalid-email message and prevents submission.
Enter an invalid phone string (letters, too short) and attempt to submit.
Confirm the form flags phone format error and prevents submission.
Enter a past date/time in the date/time fields and attempt to submit.
Verify the form rejects past dates with a clear error indicating user must choose a future appointment date.

title: Appointment form - real-time feedback and field-level validation
description: Verify field-level validation occurs as the user types/selects (real-time feedback), including success indicators or removal of error messages when corrected.

steps.description: Open the appointment form.
Type an invalid value into email, then correct it to a valid address without submitting.
Confirm the email error message is removed in real time and a success/valid indicator is optionally shown.
Select a date/time and then change it to another valid slot.
Verify any slot availability indicator updates in real time and form-level validation state reflects the change.

title: Appointment form - simulated submission and confirmation message
description: Verify that a valid appointment form triggers the simulated submission workflow and displays a user-friendly confirmation message, without actual network submission.

steps.description: Open the appointment form and fill all required fields with valid data.
Click Submit.

Confirm the UI shows a simulated submission state (e.g., spinner or 'Submitting...' message) for a short period and then displays a confirmation message indicating the appointment request was received.
Ensure the form is either reset or disabled to prevent duplicate submissions as per design, and that the confirmation message is accessible (aria-live or focus moved to message).

title: Appointment form - server error simulation handling
description: When the simulated submission returns an error state, verify the UI displays a clear error message and allows retry without losing user input.

steps.description: Open the appointment form and fill all required fields.
Trigger the simulated submission flow but force a simulated error response (e.g., toggle a debug flag or mock failure).
Check that an error banner/message is displayed with actionable copy (e.g., 'Submission failed, please try again') and that the user's entered data remains in the form fields.
Attempt to resubmit after correcting any simulated issues.
Confirm resubmission works and confirmation is shown on success.

title: Services page - list of categories and specialists presence and links
description: Verify services.html lists service categories and specialists, each entry includes a title, short description, and links to more detail or specialist profiles where applicable.

steps.description: Open services.html.
Confirm the page contains multiple service category cards/sections and each has a title and short description.
For specialist listings, verify each specialist item includes a name, role/title, and a link to view more details. Links should be clickable and navigate to detail pages or sections.
Click one service category link or specialist link.
Confirm the click navigates to the expected content without layout or styling regressions.

title: Locations page - Kerugoya and Ishiara branch data, phone and hours
description: Validate that the locations page lists both Kerugoya and Ishiara branches, each with address, operating hours in an expected format, and clickable contact links (tel: and mailto: if applicable).

steps.description: Open locations.html.
Verify that entries for Kerugoya and Ishiara are present and visible on the page.
For each branch, confirm there is an address line, contact phone number and operating hours displayed in a consistent format (e.g., Mon-Fri: 8:00–17:00).
Confirm phone numbers are anchor elements with tel: href and that clicking/tapping initiates call intent on supported devices.

title: Locations page - map/link and accessibility of contact info
description: Ensure any map links or directions links are present and accessible, and contact information uses semantic HTML for screen readers.

steps.description: Open locations.html and examine each branch card.
If a map or directions link exists, confirm it has an accessible name and target (opens in new tab with rel='noopener' if external).
Verify contact info uses appropriate semantic markup (address element or aria-labels) so screen readers can read the location details correctly.

title: Scroll-triggered reveal animations add visible class on entering viewport
description: Verify elements intended to reveal via Intersection Observer receive the visible state (e.g., 'is-visible' class) when they enter the viewport and lose it when scrolled out if intended.

steps.description: Open the homepage or a page with reveal sections.
Scroll slowly down the page to bring reveal-target elements into the viewport.
For each reveal-target element, check that the observer adds a visible class or toggles an attribute (e.g., 'is-visible' or 'data-visible=true') when the element crosses the reveal threshold.
Scroll back up and out of viewport if implementation removes visibility when out of view.
Confirm the class/attribute toggles according to the implementation spec (either persistent after first reveal or toggling) and there are no console errors from Intersection Observer.

title: Scroll animations do not cause layout shift and use transform/opacity
description: Ensure reveal animations use transform/opacity rather than layout-affecting properties to avoid layout shifts and that elements have will-change/transition optimizations.

steps.description: Open the page with reveal animations and inspect the CSS rules applied to reveal-target elements.
Confirm animation transitions animate transform and opacity properties (e.g., translateY, scale) rather than height/margin/position which cause layout shifts.
Check that elements have GPU-friendly hints (e.g., will-change: transform) and that no visible content jumps or reflows occur when reveal animations run.

title: Neumorphic design style classes and component consistency
description: Verify core UI components (buttons, cards, inputs, modals) include the expected neumorphic CSS utility classes and consistent elevation/rounded styling per design tokens.

steps.description: Open pages containing buttons, cards, inputs and modals.
Inspect these components for presence of designated neumorphic classes (e.g., 'neumorphic', 'nm-card', 'nm-button') or the expected CSS variables for shadows, radii, and background colors.
Confirm consistent border-radius and shadow tokens are applied across similar components and no component visually breaks the design system (e.g., missing background or incorrect contrast).

title: Responsive layout verification across breakpoints
description: Ensure the website layout adapts correctly across key breakpoints (mobile, tablet, desktop) without overlapping content or broken layouts.

steps.description: Open the homepage and services/appointments pages and test at multiple viewport widths: 320px, 375px, 768px, 1024px, 1366px.
Confirm header, hero, cards, forms and sliders reflow appropriately (stacking, spacing, font sizing) at each breakpoint.
Verify no horizontal scrollbars appear and no content is clipped at any breakpoint.

title: UI contrast and focus state pre-delivery checklist
description: Validate color contrast ratios for main text and interactive elements meet WCAG AA and focus states are visible per the pre-delivery accessibility checklist.

steps.description: Open multiple key pages and capture computed foreground/background color values for primary text, buttons and nav links.
Verify contrast ratio meets at least WCAG AA thresholds (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text).
Tab through interactive elements (links, buttons, inputs).
Confirm visible focus indicators are present and meet minimum size/contrast requirements (focus ring or clearly visible style).

title: Form inputs accessibility: labels, aria attributes, and error announcements
description: Verify all form controls have associated labels, required inputs use aria-required/required attribute, and validation error messages are announced to screen readers.

steps.description: Open the appointment form and inspect DOM for each input, select and textarea.
Confirm each form control has a visible label element associated via for/id or aria-label/aria-labelledby.
Trigger validation errors by submitting an empty form.
Verify error messages are connected to inputs using aria-describedby and/or appear in an aria-live region so screen readers announce them.

title: Header navigation link targets and deep-linking
description: Ensure header navigation links (internal anchors and page links) navigate to the correct sections and deep-linking preserves scroll position without breaking sticky header (i.e., content not hidden under header).

steps.description: Click header links that navigate to internal page anchors (e.g., #services, #locations) and to full pages (services.html, locations.html).

Confirm the viewport scrolls to the correct section and the top of the target content is visible (not hidden under the sticky header); if needed, confirm page applies anchor offset to account for sticky header.
Paste a deep link URL with an anchor into the address bar and load the page.
Verify the page loads and scrolls directly to the right section with header offset accounted for.

title: Slider pause on hover/focus (if implemented) and manual controls
description: If the design includes pause-on-hover or keyboard focus pause behavior for the testimonial slider, verify autoplay pauses on hover/focus and resumes after hover/focus is removed; also verify manual controls function.

steps.description: Open the homepage to the testimonial slider.
Hover the slider with the mouse and observe for one interval length.
Confirm autoplay pauses while hovered if pause-on-hover behavior is specified.
Move mouse away and wait one interval length.
Verify autoplay resumes after hover ends.
Focus the slider controls via keyboard and use them to move to next/previous slides.
Confirm manual control works and updates visible testimonial and any accessible announcements.

title: Graceful degradation when JavaScript disabled
description: Verify core content (service information, locations, contact details) and essential navigation remain accessible and readable when JavaScript is disabled (progressive enhancement).

steps.description: Disable JavaScript in the browser and load index.html and services.html and locations.html.
Confirm essential content such as service descriptions, location addresses, operation hours and phone numbers are visible as static content.

Verify navigation links still work as standard anchor links and forms (if present) fall back to server-side or provide an informative message indicating JavaScript is required for interactive submission.
